evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix is rendered correctly with diagonal/off-diagonal distinction as briefed; values annotated; rows/cols labeled C1-C10 with axis labels — readable and on-brief.
evidence.typography: Script uses default DejaVu Sans font (confirmed via PDF strings: /Type3 bitmap fonts); no pdf.fonttype=42 set anywhere, so text is not selectable/editable; font sizes are adequate but the default font is used.
evidence.palette: Blues (sequential) for off-diagonal and viridis (perceptually uniform) for diagonal are both colorblind-safe; no red/green directional misuse; dual-colormap choice is justified.
evidence.layout_density: 8x7 figure with tight_layout and colorbar; space is reasonably used for a 10x10 grid; minor wasted whitespace possible around margins but no overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption describes dual colormap encoding and axis semantics; no specific numerical finding stated (e.g., best/worst class accuracy count); a Reviewer 2 would likely ask for a quantitative claim.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as /Subtype /Type3 (bitmap outlines), not Type42 TrueType — text is not selectable; script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 setting.
evidence.file_format: All required outputs present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (25K), figure.png (64K), figure.svg (62K), caption.tex (380B), gen_figure.py (2.2K).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains rows=true/cols=predicted convention and dual colormap scheme clearly, but contains no specific numerical finding or highlighted result to anchor the reader.

overall_strengths: Correct dual-colormap design (viridis diagonal, Blues off-diagonal) that is colorblind-safe and perceptually motivated, matching the brief's hints exactly.
All required file formats are present (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption, script) and the confusion matrix layout is clear with proper axis labeling and cell annotations.
overall_weaknesses: Fonts embedded as Type3 bitmap (not Type42 TrueType) because pdf.fonttype=42 was never set — text in the PDF is not selectable or editable, a standard journal/submission requirement.
Caption lacks a concrete numerical claim or specific finding (e.g., which class had highest accuracy or the overall accuracy), making it descriptive but not scientifically informative.
